scenarios.name: Bare invocation, training-mode happy path, budget exhausted
scenarios.description: Exercises the main path: no flags, replay observation, a passing training iteration, dashboard state writes, and a complete Phase 8 morning report when the wall-clock budget runs out. Validates the banner, zero-input contract, iteration summary, morning-report tables/sections, and tag cheatsheet.
scenarios.context: Invoked as `/improve-bot-advised` with NO flags. Environment: git clean on master, in sync with origin. SC2 installed. Quality gates pass. data/checkpoints/manifest.json reports difficulty level 1. Backend port 8765 is free at start. The seed game succeeds and /a4g-dashboard-check reports all tabs green. Three replay-mode iterations run (10 games each at max speed). Iteration 1 adds an early-scout reward rule (type training, §6); win rate goes 6/10 -> 8/10, quality gates pass, Elo gate promotes -> PASS, committed with [advised-auto]. Iteration 2 tweaks chrono-boost reward weight (type training, §4); win rate 8/10 -> 7/10 (within 30% threshold) -> PASS. Iteration 3 adds a gateway-timing reward (type training, §11); win rate 7/10 -> 7/10 -> PASS. After iteration 3 the 4-hour wall-clock budget is exhausted, so the loop proceeds to Phase 8 and writes the morning report. Resolve and produce the full text the skill would emit: opening banner, per-iteration summaries, dashboard state-file contents at phase boundaries, and the final morning report.

scenarios.name: Backlog mode with Elo-gate failure and revert
scenarios.description: Exercises the conditional/edge paths: --backlog implies --self-improve-code, a dev-type iteration whose win-rate validation passes but whose Elo promotion gate fails (so it must NOT commit), and config revert on failure. Validates flag-resolution logging, backend lifecycle (daemon soak swap, never-two-servers, never-kill-SC2), and the Elo no-commit branch.
scenarios.context: Invoked as `/improve-bot-advised --backlog --observe replay --games 10 --hours 4`. The default backlog source documentation/plans/alpha4gate-master-plan.md has a `## Tactical refinements backlog` section with three `### ` items: 'T.1 — Pylon walling on natural' (Status: SHIPPED), 'T.2 — Tech structures on low ground' (no status line), 'T.3 — Late warp-prism harass' (Status: Observed). Flag resolution must log that backlog mode implies --self-improve-code, then parse the backlog: T.1 is skipped (SHIPPED), T.2 and T.3 are open, ranked by file order. Iteration 1 selects T.2 (dev type), runs /improve-bot to implement it under bots/current/, runs a daemon training soak (shut down API-only backend, start --serve --daemon, run 2 games, kill only alpha4gate --daemon processes leaving SC2_x64.exe alive, restart API-only backend, confirm port 8765 free). Validation games: win rate 7/10 -> 6/10 (PASS on WR), quality gates pass, BUT the cross-version Elo promotion gate returns promoted=False (reason: 'candidate Elo +4 below +10 threshold'). Per the skill, the iteration must NOT commit and must log the Elo-gate failure. Produce the banner, the backlog parse log, the iteration summary, and the dashboard state file for this iteration.

scenarios.name: Mode auto-downgrade and 3-consecutive-fail early stop
scenarios.description: Exercises the auto-downgrade flag rule and the early-stop-on-repeated-failure path with a hard-fail validation. Validates that '--mode both' without --self-improve-code downgrades to training-only with the literal log message, that a hard fail (degenerate behavior) is reported regardless of threshold, and that the morning report's Stop reason reflects 3 consecutive failures.
scenarios.context: Invoked as `/improve-bot-advised --mode both --games 10 --hours 6` (note: NO --self-improve-code). Flag resolution must auto-downgrade to training-only and log 'dev/both mode requires --self-improve-code, falling back to training-only' without asking the user. Three training-type reward iterations run. Iteration 1: a reward-shaping change causes the bot to stop building army units (degenerate, zero production) -> hard FAIL regardless of WR threshold, config reverted, fail_streak=1. Iteration 2: a different reward weight causes win rate 6/10 -> 2/10 (drop > 30%) -> FAIL, reverted, fail_streak=2. Iteration 3: another change causes a game crash in logs -> hard FAIL, reverted, fail_streak=3. After 3 consecutive failures the loop stops early (before budget) and proceeds to Phase 8. Produce the banner, the flag-downgrade log line, the three iteration summaries, and the morning report whose Stop reason is '3 consecutive failures'.
